questions.stem: Under ASC 715, how is the funded status of a defined benefit pension plan reported on the employer's balance sheet?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The difference between plan assets and the accumulated benefit obligation (ABO)

choices.label: B
choices.text: The difference between the fair value of plan assets and the projected benefit obligation (PBO)

choices.label: C
choices.text: Only the current year's pension expense

choices.label: D
choices.text: The vested benefit obligation

questions.explanation: ASC 715-20-45-1 requires employers to recognize the funded status of a defined benefit plan as an asset or liability. Funded status = Fair value of plan assets - Projected benefit obligation (PBO). If PBO > assets, a liability is recognized; if assets > PBO, an asset is recognized.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-20-45-1により、雇用主は確定給付制度の積立状況を資産または負債として認識することが求められます。積立状況 = 制度資産の公正価値 - 予測給付債務（PBO）。PBOが資産を超える場合は負債が認識され、資産がPBOを超える場合は資産が認識されます。

questions.stem: Which of the following is NOT a component of net periodic pension cost?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Service cost

choices.label: B
choices.text: Interest cost on the PBO

choices.label: C
choices.text: Expected return on plan assets

choices.label: D
choices.text: Contributions made by the employer to the plan

questions.explanation: The components of net periodic pension cost are: (1) service cost, (2) interest cost, (3) expected return on plan assets (reduces cost), (4) amortization of prior service cost, (5) amortization of net gain or loss, and (6) amortization of transition obligation. Employer contributions are cash flows that fund the plan but are not a component of pension expense.
questions.explanationJa: 年金費用の純額（正味期間年金費用）の構成要素は以下の通りです：(1) 勤務費用、(2) 利息費用、(3) 制度資産の期待収益率（費用を減少させる）、(4) 過去勤務費用の償却、(5) 純損益の償却、(6) 移行時債務の償却。雇用主の拠出金は制度に資金を供給するキャッシュフローであり、年金費用の構成要素ではありません。

questions.stem: Under ASU 2017-07, where should the service cost component of net periodic pension cost be presented in the income statement?

choices.label: A
choices.text: All components are presented together in operating expenses

choices.label: B
choices.text: Service cost in operating expenses; all other components outside of operating income

choices.label: C
choices.text: All components are presented in other comprehensive income

choices.label: D
choices.text: Service cost in COGS; other components in SG&A

questions.explanation: ASU 2017-07 (ASC 715-30-45-3A) requires that only the service cost component be presented in the same income statement line as other employee compensation costs (operating expenses). All other components (interest cost, expected return, amortizations) must be presented separately from service cost and outside of operating income.
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2017-07（ASC 715-30-45-3A）では、勤務費用のみが他の従業員報酬費用と同じ損益計算書の項目（営業費用）に表示されることが求められます。その他すべての構成要素（利息費用、期待収益、償却額）は勤務費用とは別に、営業利益の外に表示しなければなりません。

questions.stem: When a pension plan is amended to provide increased benefits for past service, the resulting prior service cost is:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Recognized immediately in pension expense

choices.label: B
choices.text: Recognized in other comprehensive income (OCI) and amortized to expense over the remaining service period of active employees

choices.label: C
choices.text: Charged directly to retained earnings

choices.label: D
choices.text: Added to goodwill

questions.explanation: Under ASC 715-20-35-24, prior service cost arising from a plan amendment is initially recognized in OCI. It is then amortized from accumulated OCI to net periodic pension cost over the future service periods of active employees expected to receive benefits. The amortization typically uses a straight-line method over the average remaining service period.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-20-35-24に基づき、制度改定により生じた過去勤務費用は、まずその他の包括利益（OCI）に認識されます。その後、給付を受けることが見込まれる現役従業員の将来の勤務期間にわたり、累積OCIから正味期間年金費用へ償却されます。償却は通常、平均残存勤務期間にわたる定額法が用いられます。

questions.stem: Under the corridor approach for amortizing net gains/losses, amortization is required when the unrecognized net gain or loss exceeds:

choices.label: A
choices.text: 5% of the greater of PBO or plan assets

choices.label: B
choices.text: 10% of the greater of PBO or market-related value of plan assets

choices.label: C
choices.text: 15% of the PBO

choices.label: D
choices.text: 20% of plan assets

questions.explanation: Under ASC 715-30-35-24 (the corridor approach), amortization of the net gain or loss is required only if the cumulative unrecognized net gain or loss exceeds 10% of the greater of the PBO or the market-related value of plan assets at the beginning of the year. The excess is amortized over the average remaining service period of active employees.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-30-35-24（回廊アプローチ）に基づき、累積未認識純損益がPBOまたは制度資産の市場関連価値のいずれか大きい方の10%を超える場合にのみ、純損益の償却が必要となります。超過額は現役従業員の平均残存勤務期間にわたって償却されます。

questions.stem: What is the primary difference between the projected benefit obligation (PBO) and the accumulated benefit obligation (ABO)?

choices.label: A
choices.text: PBO includes assumed future salary increases; ABO does not

choices.label: B
choices.text: ABO includes future salary increases; PBO does not

choices.label: C
choices.text: PBO uses a higher discount rate

choices.label: D
choices.text: There is no difference; they are synonymous

questions.explanation: The PBO measures the obligation using projected (expected future) salary levels, while the ABO measures the obligation based on current salary levels only. The PBO is used for pension expense computation and balance sheet recognition. The ABO is used for the additional minimum liability disclosure requirement.
questions.explanationJa: PBO（予測給付債務）は将来の予測給与水準を用いて債務を測定する一方、ABO（累積給付債務）は現在の給与水準のみに基づいて債務を測定します。PBOは年金費用の計算および貸借対照表での認識に使用されます。ABOは追加最低負債の開示要件に使用されます。

questions.stem: A plan curtailment occurs when an event significantly reduces the expected years of future service of active employees. The effect of a curtailment on prior service cost is:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Ignored until employees actually leave

choices.label: B
choices.text: Recognized immediately in earnings for the portion of prior service cost related to the reduced future service

choices.label: C
choices.text: Deferred and amortized over 10 years

choices.label: D
choices.text: Recognized in OCI only

questions.explanation: Under ASC 715-30-35-92, a curtailment results in immediate recognition in earnings of the portion of prior service cost (from accumulated OCI) associated with the years of future service no longer expected to be rendered. Additionally, any curtailment gain or loss from the change in PBO is recognized.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-30-35-92に基づき、縮小（カーテイルメント）が発生した場合、今後提供されることが見込まれなくなった将来勤務年数に関連する過去勤務費用の部分（累積OCIから）が、損益として即時に認識されます。さらに、PBOの変動から生じる縮小損益も認識されます。

questions.stem: Other postretirement employee benefits (OPEB), such as retiree health care, differ from pensions in that:

choices.label: A
choices.text: OPEB plans are always funded; pensions are not

choices.label: B
choices.text: OPEB plans are generally not funded; the obligation is called the APBO (accumulated postretirement benefit obligation)

choices.label: C
choices.text: OPEB uses the same accounting as pensions with no differences

choices.label: D
choices.text: OPEB obligations are never reported on the balance sheet

questions.explanation: OPEB plans (ASC 715-60) are generally unfunded (no plan assets), unlike pension plans which typically have a trust holding plan assets. The obligation is measured as the APBO (accumulated postretirement benefit obligation). The general accounting framework is similar to pensions (recognize funded status, OCI items), but the lack of funding makes the liability typically larger relative to assets.
questions.explanationJa: OPEB制度（ASC 715-60）は一般的に積立てられていません（制度資産なし）。これは通常、信託に制度資産を保有する年金制度とは異なります。債務はAPBO（累積退職後給付債務）として測定されます。一般的な会計フレームワークは年金と類似していますが（積立状況の認識、OCI項目）、資金の積立てがないため、資産に対して負債が通常大きくなります。

questions.stem: A pension plan has plan assets with a fair value of $2,000,000 and uses a market-related value of $1,800,000. The expected long-term rate of return is 7%. What is the expected return on plan assets used in computing pension expense?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $140,000 (based on fair value)

choices.label: B
choices.text: $126,000 (based on market-related value)

choices.label: C
choices.text: $133,000 (average of fair value and market-related value)

choices.label: D
choices.text: The actual return on plan assets

questions.explanation: Under ASC 715-30-35-22, the expected return on plan assets is calculated by multiplying the expected long-term rate of return by the market-related value of plan assets (which can be either fair value or a calculated value that smooths short-term market fluctuations over up to 5 years). Expected return = $1,800,000 × 7% = $126,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-30-35-22に基づき、制度資産の期待収益は、長期期待収益率に制度資産の市場関連価値を乗じて計算されます（市場関連価値は公正価値、または最大5年間にわたる短期的な市場変動を平準化した計算値のいずれか）。期待収益 = $1,800,000 × 7% = $126,000となります。

questions.stem: Which of the following must be disclosed for defined benefit pension plans?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The names of all plan participants

choices.label: B
choices.text: A reconciliation of the beginning and ending balances of both the PBO and the fair value of plan assets

choices.label: C
choices.text: Only the total pension expense for the year

choices.label: D
choices.text: Individual account balances for each employee

questions.explanation: ASC 715-20-50-1 requires extensive disclosures including: a reconciliation of PBO (showing service cost, interest cost, actuarial gains/losses, benefits paid, amendments), a reconciliation of plan assets (contributions, actual return, benefits paid), the components of net periodic pension cost, and the assumptions used (discount rate, expected return, salary growth rate).
questions.explanationJa: ASC 715-20-50-1では、広範な開示が求められます。これには、PBOの調整表（勤務費用、利息費用、数理計算上の損益、給付支払額、制度改定を表示）、制度資産の調整表（拠出金、実際収益、給付支払額）、正味期間年金費用の構成要素、および使用した前提条件（割引率、期待収益率、昇給率）が含まれます。
